title: (DOC) The Medici and Florence - Academia.edu
highlights:
Medici family is intricately connected to the history of Florence during the Italian Renaissance, serving as a powerful force in the city's politics, economy, and culture. This paper explores the rise of the Medici, particularly under Cosimo de' Medici, and examines their dual legacy of fostering artistic advancements and contributing to political turmoil, challenging the notions of republican freedoms in Florence. title: THE REVOLUTION OF 1494 IN FLORENCE AND ITS AFTERMATH
highlights:
Publication: Medicean and Savonarolan Florence, pp 115-138 - Publisher: Brepols
- Publication Date: January 2011  ...    This volume examines Florentine society at crucial moments of change that are often treated separately in historical narratives: the later years of Medici government under the aegis of Lorenzo the Magnificent, the four tumultuous years of Savonarola’s religious regime from 1494 to 1498, and the unsettled early decades of the sixteenth century. Drawing upon original research conducted during the past decade, it provides important insights into the politics and conflicting ideologies in the city as experienced by different levels
